metadata.title: Route Any Task to the Right Claude Surface in Thirty Seconds

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Aloha, Liam — in for Bear. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Build it with a CLI, then take it apart?
props.segment: Route Any Task to the Right

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

Five questions, thirty seconds, and you never open the wrong Claude window again. Most people pick a surface by habit — chat for everything — and wonder why the output cannot be trusted. The routing table is not a suggestion; it is runnable code.

shot.notes: title card: 'Five questions. Thirty seconds. Never open the wrong Claude surface again.' — hook on dark ground, CRIMSON accent on 'wrong'

beats.narration_text: Open the terminal. Ask Claude to build a routing advisor: a CLI that reads a task description, evaluates five dimensions — output type, context source, risk, reversibility, verification path — and prints a surface recommendation with a one-sentence rationale.

props.command:
claude "Build routing_advisor.py: reads a task description
from stdin, evaluates five dimensions:
  output_type (text/code/file)
  context_source (conversation/repo/filesystem)
  risk_level (low/medium/high)
  reversibility (reversible/irreversible)
  verification_path (domain-check/tests/manual)
Print recommended surface and determining factor."

beats.narration_text: Read the code before running it. The five dimensions map to a scoring matrix — output type and context source place you on the surface axis, then risk and reversibility gate the result. If any dimension scores human-only, that wins regardless of the others.

Run it with three tasks from chapter one's routing table. Write a summary — chat. Fix a bug in auth dot py — Code. Synthesize a folder of program notes — Cowork. Each routes instantly, each rationale is one line.

shot.notes: three-row terminal output: task 1 -> claude-ai / context_source=conversation; task 2 -> claude-code / context_source=repo; task 3 -> claude-cowork / context_source=filesystem — color-coded rows

beats.narration_text: Now add the risk override. Any task tagged irreversible AND high-risk routes to human-only, regardless of every other dimension. One flag, one guarantee.

props.command:
claude "update routing_advisor.py:
  add --risk-override flag
  irreversible + high-risk -> human-only
  regardless of all other dimensions
  verify with: delete production database"

beats.narration_text: Test the override. 'Delete the production database' — irreversible plus high-risk — routes to human-only. The determining factor prints on one line: risk override triggered. Every other dimension was overruled.

shot.notes: terminal: echo 'delete the production database' | python3 routing_advisor.py --risk-override — output: Surface: human-only / Factor: risk override triggered — CRIMSON highlight on 'human-only'

beats.narration_text: Routing by habit is the most common Claude failure mode. Everything to chat, regardless of where the context lives. The five-question filter takes under a minute — it prevents hours of underpowered or dangerous delegation.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'Routing by habit → context mismatch → unverifiable output' (CRIMSON) vs 'Five-question filter → right surface → trustworthy output' (teal #2A7A2A)

beats.narration_text: Your move: extend the five dimensions into a YAML config and run your last ten Claude tasks through it. Next reel: the six-component specification that turns weak prompts into auditable artifacts.

shot.notes: next-reel teaser card: 'Next: Six-Component Prompt Specification' — segment 02 of the CLAUDE series

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Route Any Task to the Right Claude Surface in Thirty Seconds] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
